Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the Carmen Reyes thread about Jae-won Kim's Austin lease and the guarantor packet request. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Carmen guarantor or Jae-won lease or Barton Creek or UrbanNest guarantor. Carmen emailed Suki on April 8 flagging that the landlord at Barton Creek Flats (Unit 5-214, 2414 South Lamar, Austin) would not countersign without a corporate guarantor packet: MoveOps entity name, certificate of good standing, authorized signer contact, and proof of business address. Jae-won was in temporary housing through April 12. Suki replied April 9 promising the packet by end of week but never sent it.
OE 2: Search emails for Suki Patel's own sent messages about the Jae-won situation to trace the promise and whether any follow-through happened. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Jae-won or guarantor or Barton Creek, optionally with folder_name SENT. Suki's reply to Carmen (April 9) promised the packet by end of week. Suki's later admission email to Mina and Chloe confirms she never sent it because the Deshawn Hartley trade show deadline consumed that week. Suki also acknowledges she closed the Linear ticket and left Airtable showing Completed during the StormCloud batch closeout around April 10.
OE 3: Search emails for Jae-won Kim's own follow-up to MoveOps about his lease status. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Jae-won lease paperwork or Jae-won apartment. Jae-won emailed support@moveops.com on April 15 asking for his final lease documents, not knowing the lease was never executed. Omar Ibrahim replied saying he would check with the relocation team but triaged the request as low-priority because Airtable showed Completed.
OE 4: Search emails for Liam Park's escalation about StormCloud failures. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Liam Park or StormCloud escalation or leadership meeting. Liam Park (StormCloud CFO) emailed Mina on April 24 with a multi-issue complaint before a leadership meeting: Jae-won's unsigned lease and expiring temp housing, a $500 goodwill credit that never appeared on the invoice, Simone Richter's standing desk reimbursement stuck in limbo, and Anya Petrova's referral to Ridgeline Robotics that went unanswered.
OE 5: Search emails for Mina Hashimoto's reconstruction and recovery plan sent to Liam Park. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Mina recovery plan or Mina Liam StormCloud. Mina sent a detailed recovery email to Liam promising to reopen Jae-won's case immediately, send the guarantor packet to UrbanNest before close that day, extend temp housing, investigate the credit and stipend issues, and follow up on Ridgeline. Mina also sent an internal email asking Suki, Chloe, Marcus, Julian, and David to confirm their respective sections.
OE 6: Search Slack in the operations channel for discussions about the Jae-won lease failure, Suki's admission, and Chloe's audit findings. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like Jae-won lease or guarantor packet or StormCloud audit, optionally with filter_in_channel set to C006. Chloe posted her ops review confirming Carmen flagged the missing guarantor packet on April 8, Suki replied April 9 promising to send it, no evidence the packet ever went out, and the false completion signals in Linear and Airtable caused the downstream support misprioritization.
OE 7: Search Airtable for Jae-won Kim's relocation record to check its current status. Use airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Jae-won Kim. The record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) shows Status as In Progress (previously showed Completed incorrectly, was corrected during the April 24 reconstruction), Origin Seattle WA, Destination Austin TX, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel, with Special Requirements noting the unsigned lease and guarantor holdout.
OE 8: Search Linear for Jae-won Kim's relocation tracking ticket and the StormCloud reconstruction issue. Use linear_list_issues with query containing keywords like Jae-won or StormCloud or lease. The agent discovers the relocation tracking ticket (closed prematurely during the batch closeout around April 10), the support follow-up issue about Jae-won's April 15 email (triaged as low-priority by Omar based on false Airtable status), and the reconstruction issue opened by Mina during the April 24 recovery.
OE 9: Search emails for the Simone Richter standing desk reimbursement situation as part of the StormCloud fallout. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Simone Richter or standing desk StormCloud or stipend reimbursement Simone. Simone submitted a $340 standing desk expense on March 25 that is stuck under the blanket stipend freeze from the fraud review. Mina flagged it to Marcus as legitimate on April 14 but no resolution visible.
OE 10: Search emails or Slack for the Ridgeline Robotics referral that went unanswered as part of the StormCloud fallout. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Ridgeline or Anya Petrova or Kenji Watanabe, or conversations_search_messages with search_query containing Ridgeline or referral. Anya Petrova from StormCloud sent a warm introduction to David Chen on April 18 connecting MoveOps with Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for a 5-employee Austin relocation. David celebrated the referral in Slack but no CRM lead was created and Kenji was not contacted. Kenji followed up on April 21 still unanswered. During the April 24 recovery, David confirmed he would create CRM records and reply.
OE 11: Search emails or Slack for the $500 goodwill credit issue as part of the StormCloud fallout. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like goodwill credit StormCloud or $500 credit or EST-2026-0287. Marcus confirmed the $500 credit from the earlier flight booking error (Jamie Reeves wrong date) was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice INV-2026-0319. StormCloud never saw it on their bill. Finance needs to move the credit to the correct document.
OE 12: Look up contact details for Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest before sending the guarantor email. Use contacts_search_contacts with query Carmen Reyes or Carmen. Carmen Reyes is the UrbanNest housing contact, email carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com.
OE 13: Look up contact details for Chloe Vance and Mina Hashimoto before sending the briefing. Use contacts_search_contacts with query Chloe Vance and contacts_search_contacts with query Mina Hashimoto. Chloe Vance is the Operations Manager (chloe.vance@moveops.com), Mina Hashimoto is the Account Manager for StormCloud and BrightLoop (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com).
OE 14: Update Jae-won Kim's Airtable relocation record with corrected status information. Use airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01, records including the record ID for Jae-won with updated Special Requirements documenting the active recovery and guarantor packet status. The record may already show In Progress from the April 24 correction but needs updated notes reflecting the April 26 actions.
OE 15: Reopen or update the Linear relocation ticket for Jae-won Kim to reflect the active crisis. Use linear_update_issue with the relocation issue id, setting state to In Progress or a reopened state with Urgent priority and updated description documenting the full failure timeline and current recovery status.
OE 16: Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest with the corporate guarantor packet information. Use send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com, recipients including carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com. Content should include the four items the landlord requested: MoveOps entity name as guarantor, certificate of good standing reference, authorized signer contact, and proof of business address. Should also ask Carmen to confirm whether Unit 5-214 is still available and what Jae-won's current temporary housing status is.
OE 17: Send an email to Chloe Vance and Mina Hashimoto with the full picture of the Jae-won situation and the broader StormCloud fallout. Use send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com, recipients including chloe.vance@moveops.com and mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Content should cover the complete lease timeline from April 8 to present, confirmation that the guarantor was never sent until today, the actions taken to fix records, the broader StormCloud fallout items from Liam Park's escalation ($500 credit, Simone stipend, Ridgeline referral), and what still needs human action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
